impact: The daemon now has narrow fixture-first and validation-first work queued, allowing it to continue toward public crawling, extraction, process modeling, guardrails, and mocked DevHub workflow support without touching live private automation or application artifacts.
summary: Appended the next ordered PP&D backlog tranche to the daemon task board while preserving all completed tasks.
target_task: Supervisor repair: all PP&D daemon tasks are complete; review the original PP&D goal and add the next task-board tranche

command: files=$(find ppd -name '*.ts' -not -path '*/node_modules/*' -print); test -z "$files" || npx tsc --noEmit --target ES2020 --module ESNext --moduleResolution node --strict --skipLibCheck --types node $files

impact: This gives the supervisor a deterministic recovery policy after repeated Python and TypeScript compile failures: classify syntax failures separately, force smaller proposals, require changed-file syntax checks before full validation, and avoid broad contract rewrites until the daemon is producing syntactically valid patches again.
summary: Add supervisor repair guidance for syntax-failure recovery in PP&D daemon cycles.
target_task: Supervisor repair: 2 recent validation rollbacks were syntax or compile failures; patch daemon prompt, preflight, or repair guidance before more worker attempts

impact: This gives the supervisor deterministic recovery rules for SyntaxError, py_compile, and TypeScript parser failures: stop broad retries, shrink the next worker proposal, require syntax-first validation, and keep live/authenticated work out of recovery attempts until compile-clean fixture work passes.

target_task: Supervisor repair: 2 recent validation rollbacks were syntax or compile failures; patch daemon prompt, preflight, or repair guidance before more worker attempts
